findings.claim: Decision.10 states retention is a 'four-class, role-based model (§7.1)', but §7.1's own class (4) reads 'Operational track-record — not an RG class; its posture is §5.4's archival demotion.' Decision.10 additionally frames the model as Reasoning-Graph retention ('Reasoning-Graph retention is a four-class, role-based model'), yet only three of the four are RG retention classes; the fourth is explicitly declared not an RG class. The Decision enumeration overstates the RG model it points to.

cited_authority.ref: DDR-003 Decision.10 ('four-class, role-based model') vs §7.1 class (4) ('not an RG class')

findings.authority_locus: DDR-003 §7.1 class (4) text ('not an RG class; its posture is §5.4's archival demotion') + the deliberation record Obligation 4 (four retention classes, class 4 = Operational archival posture, a pointer)

findings.claim: §4 lists 'working-tier retention windows (§7.1)' as a single named calibration parameter and §4.3 validates 'every §7.1 working-tier retention window' against lookbacks, implying multiple windows. §7.1 class (3) states 'bounded retention windows, values deferred (§4)' — plural. Additionally §7.2 states class-2 Evidence 'is exempt from expiry ... until a survival mechanism ... lands' — whether that exemption is itself a §4 parameter or a fixed interim is not declared in §4's parameter set. The retention-parameter decision is stated at differing cardinality and scope across §4 and §7, so a reader cannot determine what the §4 mechanism actually governs.

cited_authority.ref: DDR-003 §4 (single 'working-tier retention windows' parameter) vs §7.1/§7.2 (plural class-3 windows; class-2 exemption scope undeclared)

findings.claim: Checked the remedy-boundary ruling for scope overreach — whether it re-authors supersession/retraction mechanics owned upstream, and whether §6.2's re-admission rule asserts consumption behavior the landed schema does not support. It held: §6.1 fixes only the replaceability-first / conjunctive-retraction policy test (ADR-008 §2.6 routes exactly this here by name), records the defect-at-promotion-vs-world-moved axis as informing-only ('never decides the remedy'), and executes on landed mechanics (DDR-002 §7 #21/#22, SDD-001 §3.5.3–§3.5.4) without re-authoring them. §6.2 keys read-exclusion on 'the executed `RETRACTS` edge exactly as landed' (DDR-002 §5) and routes re-admission through a fresh forward EA-gated re-promotion, adding no read-time verdict evaluation.

cited_authority.ref: ADR-008 §2.6 (remedy boundary routed to feedback-loop governance) + DDR-002 §5 / §7 #21 (retraction read-discipline, edge-keyed exclusion) — DDR-003 §6.1/§6.2 conform

findings.claim: Checked the two-distinct-controls seam and the distillation-reviewer role model for stance leakage into ADR-008's principle layer and for a downstream carve-out permitting a non-human holder of a control ADR-008 fixes as human. It held: §1.2 confines itself to policy (which control governs which write), explicitly states 'not a gated/ungated split,' and does not re-litigate the control-types ADR-008 §2.2 fixes; §1.3 fences the human-review constraint honestly — 'ADR-008 §2.2 fixes both controls as *human* review ... any future non-human role-holder is therefore an amendment question for that upstream authority, not an assignment act under this record' — so no downstream qualifier rescues a non-human holder past the upstream commitment.

cited_authority.ref: ADR-008 §2.2 (promotion + operational-distillation control-types, both human review with a named human answerable) + §2.6 (governance policy routed here) — DDR-003 §1.2/§1.3 conform
